description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese sword set displayed on a black wooden stand. The set includes two swords, likely a katana and a wakizashi, both sheathed in black lacquered scabbards with subtle gold and brown decorative accents near the handles. The handles are wrapped in a textured black material, possibly ray skin and cord, with gold embellishments at the ends and guards. The stand itself is simple and elegant, with a glossy finish and a gold decorative crest or emblem on the front. The swords are positioned horizontally, one above the other, with the blades fully enclosed in their scabbards. The overall appearance is refined and ceremonial, suitable for display in a home, dojo, or collector's setting.
description: A pair of traditional Japanese swords, sheathed and displayed on a glossy black stand with gold accents and decorative handles.
description_view_0: Side view showing both swords sheathed and resting on the black stand, with handle and scabbard details visible.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the narrow profile of the stand and swords, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the gold crest on the stand and the decorative elements on the scabbards and handles.
description_view_3: Rear end-on view showing the back of the stand and the ends of the sword handles.
attribute_handle_design: wrapped, straight, black cord with gold decorative end caps
attribute_ornamentation: gold accents and crest, decorative end caps
